overview: Atal Innovation Mission (AIM) is Government of India’s flagship initiative to create and promote a culture of innovation and entrepreneurship across the country. AIM’s objective is to develop new programmes and policies for fostering innovation in different sectors of the economy, provide platforms and collaboration opportunities for different stakeholders, and create an umbrella structure to oversee the innovation & entrepreneurship ecosystem of the country. It was set up in 2016 by NITI Aayog and has launched multiple programs including Atal Tinkering Labs (ATL), Atal Incubation Centres (AIC), Atal Community Innovation Centres (ACIC), Atal New India Challenges (ANIC), and others to support innovation at school, university, and enterprise levels.
objectives: Develop new programmes and policies for fostering innovation in different sectors of the economy
Provide platforms and collaboration opportunities for different stakeholders
Create an umbrella structure to oversee the innovation & entrepreneurship ecosystem of the country
Foster curiosity, creativity, and imagination in young minds through Atal Tinkering Labs
Promote innovation and entrepreneurship among young innovators through Atal Incubation Centres
Support technology-based innovations solving national challenges through Atal New India Challenge
Encourage entrepreneurship in underserved/unserved regions through Atal Community Innovation Centres
Build capacity in vernacular languages through the Vernacular Innovation Program
Support deep technology startups through AIM-PRIME program
Enable global collaboration through RISE Accelerator with Australia
eligibility: Eligibility varies by sub-program: For Atal Tinkering Labs (ATL), schools across India can apply; for Atal Incubation Centres (AIC), universities, institutions, and corporates can apply; for Atal Community Innovation Centres (ACIC), applicants must be from underserved regions with partnership and matching funding; for Atal New India Challenge (ANIC), Indian companies (MSMEs or DPIIT-recognized startups) with a working prototype and majority Indian stakeholding are eligible; for Youth Co:Lab, youth-led startups with founders aged 15-29 (extended to 35 for women, PwDs, LGBTIQA+, tribal, and minority entrepreneurs) can apply; for AIM-PRIME, technology developers, incubation managers, scientists, and academicians with science-based deep tech ideas are eligible.
benefits: Benefits include access to state-of-the-art infrastructure (e.g., ATLs with IoT, 3D printing, robotics), mentorship from 6200+ Mentors of Change, funding support (up to INR 1 crore under ANIC, up to INR 2.5 crores for ACIC with matching), co-working spaces, lab facilities, networking opportunities, technical and business support, prototype development assistance, commercialization support, access to global ecosystems (e.g., RISE Accelerator), seed grants (INR 2.2–3.5 lakhs in Youth Co:Lab), skill development in design thinking and entrepreneurship, exposure to investors and markets, and recognition through national platforms and awards.
financial_support: AIM provides financial support through various programs: Atal New India Challenge (ANIC) offers grant-in-aid of up to INR 1 crore for startups over 12–18 months; Atal Community Innovation Centres (ACIC) receive up to INR 2.5 crores from AIM subject to equal or greater matching funding from partners; Youth Co:Lab National Innovation Challenge provides seed grants of INR 2.2–3.5 lakhs to top 6 startups; AIM-PRIME offers guidance and training but no direct financial grant; Atal Tinkering Labs receive grant-in-aid for establishment (amount not specified in evidence); Fund of Funds for Startups (FFS) of INR 10,000 Cr is managed by SIDBI (mentioned in context but not directly under AIM).
application_process: 1. Visit the official AIM website (aim.gov.in) to check for open applications under specific programs (ATL, AIC, ACIC, ANIC, etc.). 2. Note that application portals for ATL, AIC, and ACIC are currently not open (as per notice on homepage). 3. For open programs, register on the designated portal (e.g., atl.aim.gov.in for ATL). 4. Fill out the application form with required details (school/institution info, project proposal, etc.). 5. Upload necessary documents (certificates, prototypes, team details, etc.). 6. Submit the application and await evaluation. 7. Shortlisted candidates may be invited for interviews, presentations, or field verification. 8. Final selection is communicated via email or portal. 9. Selected entities receive grant-in-aid and must submit utilization certificates and progress reports. 10. Post-selection, beneficiaries gain access to mentorship, infrastructure, and ecosystem support.
deadlines: Rolling basis — application portals for ATL, AIC, and ACIC are not currently open; specific programs like Youth Co:Lab have fixed deadlines (e.g., apply before midnight on 20th March 2026 for Youth Co:Lab 2026).
implementing_agency: Atal Innovation Mission (AIM), NITI Aayog, Government of India
caveats: Application portal for ATL, AIC, and ACIC are not currently open as per notice on aim.gov.in homepage
Financial support under ACIC requires equal or greater matching funding from partners
ANIC support is limited to prototype-stage innovations and includes mentorship and ecosystem access alongside funding
Youth Co:Lab eligibility includes age relaxation for women, PwDs, LGBTIQA+, tribal, and minority entrepreneurs (up to 35 years)
AIM-PRIME program does not provide direct financial grants but offers training, mentorship, and guidance
Fund utilization requires submission of Utilization Certificates and periodic progress reports
Beneficiaries must comply with monitoring and evaluation frameworks (e.g., MyATL Dashboard for ATLs)
